svg.title: REALM Stage 2 gradient-reversal architecture
svg.desc: EEG input enters a CNN-Transformer backbone and produces latent vector z. A solid blue branch sends z to a seizure head that minimizes seizure loss. A dashed orange branch sends z through a gradient reversal layer and patient head. The patient head minimizes positive cross-entropy with respect to its own parameters, while the reversed gradient causes only the backbone to maximize patient loss. The total backbone objective is seizure loss minus lambda times patient loss.

cc:Work.dc:description: EEG input enters a CNN-Transformer backbone and produces latent vector z. A solid blue branch sends z to a seizure head that minimizes seizure loss. A dashed orange branch sends z through a gradient reversal layer and patient head. The patient head minimizes positive cross-entropy with respect to its own parameters, while the reversed gradient causes only the backbone to maximize patient loss. The total backbone objective is seizure loss minus lambda times patient loss.

g.text: For the backbone, the patient loss is adversarially maximized while seizure loss is minimized.

text.style: font-weight: 700; font-size: 18px; font-family: 'DejaVu Sans'; text-anchor: start; fill: #17324d
font-size: 10.5px; font-family: 'DejaVu Sans'; text-anchor: start; fill: #687381

g.text: The seizure path preserves pathology; the gradient-reversal path suppresses patient-identifying information.

text.style: font-weight: 700; font-size: 8.5px; font-family: 'DejaVu Sans'; text-anchor: end; fill: #d97706
